nodes.name: Flores green pigeon
nodes.summary: The Flores green pigeon (Treron floris) is a species of bird in the family Columbidae. It is endemic to the Lesser Sunda Islands of Indonesia and occur on the islands Lombok, Sumbawa, Flores, Solor, Lembata, Pantar and Alor. Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical dry forests, subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests, and dry savanna. It is threatened by habitat loss. The Flores green pigeon feeds primarily on fruit and therefore a forest habitat is essential for its survival. Its relatively small population qualifies this species to be categorized as a "Vulnerable" one. While hunting poses somewhat of a threat to the Flores green pigeon, its primary threat remains as the loss of habitat due to the burning of vegetation during the dry season. References Pire, John. "Flores Green Pigeon." International Dove Society. 4 Feb. 2008 External links BirdLife Species Factsheet.

nodes.name: 弗岛绿鸠
nodes.summary: 弗岛绿鸠（Treron floris）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联1种鸟类，如Flores green pigeon。

nodes.name: 绿鸠属
nodes.summary: 绿鸠属（Treron）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联27种鸟类，如小绿鸠、红颈绿鸠、棕头绿鸠等27种鸟类。
